$comment: Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/skills#frontmatter-reference — Check this page periodically for new fields.
title: Skill Frontmatter Schema
description: Schema for Claude Code skill (SKILL.md) and command (.claude/commands/*.md) YAML frontmatter fields. Skills and commands share the same frontmatter spec.

name.description: Display name for the skill. If omitted, uses the directory name. Lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens only (max 64 characters).

description.description: What the skill does and when to use it. Claude uses this to decide when to apply the skill. Descriptions longer than 250 characters are truncated.

argument-hint.description: Hint shown during autocomplete to indicate expected arguments. Example: '[issue-number]' or '[filename] [format]'.

disable-model-invocation.description: Set to true to prevent Claude from automatically loading this skill. Use for workflows you want to trigger manually with /name. Default: false.

user-invocable.description: Set to false to hide from the / menu. Use for background knowledge users shouldn't invoke directly. Default: true.

allowed-tools.description: Tools Claude can use without asking permission when this skill is active. Comma-separated string or array.

model.description: Model to use when this skill is active.

effort.description: Effort level when this skill is active. Overrides the session effort level.

context.description: Set to 'fork' to run in a forked subagent context.

agent.description: Which subagent type to use when context: fork is set. Options include built-in agents (Explore, Plan, general-purpose) or custom subagents. Default: general-purpose.

hooks.description: Hooks scoped to this skill's lifecycle. Same format as hooks in settings.json.

paths.description: Glob patterns that limit when this skill is activated. Comma-separated string or YAML list. When set, Claude loads the skill automatically only when working with matching files.

shell.description: Shell to use for !`command` blocks in this skill. Accepts 'bash' (default) or 'powershell'.
